heading: Basic energy sciences
label: 18641 Basic energy sciences

children.heading: Program
children.label: a Program
children.text: As part of the activities authorized under , the Director shall carry out a research and development program in basic energy sciences, including materials sciences and engineering, chemical sciences, physical biosciences, geosciences, and other disciplines, to understand, model, and control matter and energy at the electronic, atomic, and molecular levels in order to provide the foundations for new energy technologies, address scientific grand challenges, and support the energy, environment, and national security missions of the Department.section 7139 of this title

children.heading: Sustainable chemistry
children.label: b Sustainable chemistry
children.text: In carrying out chemistry-related research and development activities under this section, the Director shall prioritize research and development of sustainable chemistry to support clean, safe, and economic alternatives and methodologies to traditional chemical products and processes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall carry out a program to provide awards, on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis, to multi-institutional collaborations or other appropriate entities to conduct fundamental and use-inspired energy research to accelerate scientific breakthroughs.

children.heading: Collaborations
children.label: 2 Collaborations
children.text: A collaboration receiving an award under this subsection may include multiple types of institutions and private sector entities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A collaboration under this subsection shall be selected for a period of 4 years.

children.heading: Existing centers
children.label: B Existing centers
children.text: An Energy Frontier Research Center in existence and supported by the Director on , may continue to receive support for a period of 4 years beginning on the date of establishment of that center.September 28, 2018

children.heading: Reapplication
children.label: C Reapplication
children.text: After the end of the period described in subparagraph (A) or (B), as applicable, a recipient of an award may reapply for selection on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: D Termination
children.text: Consistent with the existing authorities of the Department, the Director may terminate an underperforming center for cause during the performance period.

children.heading: No funding for construction
children.label: 4 No funding for construction
children.text: No funding provided pursuant to this subsection may be used for the construction of new buildings or facilities.

children.heading: Basic energy sciences user facilities
children.label: d Basic energy sciences user facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall carry out a program for the development, construction, operation, and maintenance of national user facilities.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 2 Requirements
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the national user facilities developed, constructed, operated, or maintained under paragraph (1) shall serve the needs of the Department, industry, the academic community, and other relevant entities to create and examine materials and chemical processes for the purpose of improving the competitiveness of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: x-ray light sources;

children.label: B
children.text: neutron sources;

children.label: C
children.text: nanoscale science research centers;

children.label: D
children.text: autonomous chemistry and materials synthesis and characterization facilities that leverage advances in artificial intelligence; and

children.label: E
children.text: such other facilities as the Director considers appropriate, consistent with .section 7139 of this title

children.heading: Flux
children.label: i Flux
children.text: The term “flux” means the rate of flow of photons.

children.heading: Hard x-ray
children.label: ii Hard x-ray
children.text: The term “hard x-ray” means a photon with energy greater than 20 kiloelectron volts.

children.heading: Upgrade
children.label: B Upgrade
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the upgrade to the Advanced Photon Source described in the publication approved by the Basic Energy Sciences Advisory Committee on , entitled “Report on Facility Upgrades”, including the development of a multibend achromat lattice to produce a high flux of coherent x-rays within the hard x-ray energy region and a suite of beamlines optimized for this source.June 9, 2016

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: C Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the upgrade under this paragraph occurs before .March 31, 2026

children.heading: Funding
children.label: D Funding
children.text: Out of funds authorized to be appropriated under subsection (j), there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the upgrade under this paragraph $14,200,000 for fiscal year 2023.

children.heading: Spallation Neutron Source proton power upgrade
children.label: 5 Spallation Neutron Source proton power upgrade

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the proton power upgrade to the Spallation Neutron Source.

children.label: i
children.text: the publication entitled “Facilities for the Future of Science: A Twenty-Year Outlook”, published by the Office of Science of the Department in December, 2003;

children.label: ii
children.text: the publication entitled “Four Years Later: An Interim Report on Facilities for the Future of Science: A Twenty-Year Outlook”, published by the Office of Science of the Department in August, 2007; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the publication approved by the Basic Energy Sciences Advisory Committee on , entitled “Report on Facility Upgrades”.June 9, 2016

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: C Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the upgrade under this paragraph occurs before , with the option for early operation in 2025.July 30, 2028

children.label: i
children.text: $17,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: ii

children.label: iii

children.heading: Spallation Neutron Source second target station
children.label: 6 Spallation Neutron Source second target station

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for a second target station for the Spallation Neutron Source.

children.label: i
children.text: the publication entitled, “Facilities for the Future of Science: A Twenty-Year Outlook”, published by the Office of Science of the Department in December, 2003;

children.label: ii
children.text: the publication entitled, “Four Years Later: An Interim Report on Facilities for the Future of Science: A Twenty-Year Outlook”, published by the Office of Science of the Department in August, 2007; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the publication approved by the Basic Energy Sciences Advisory Committee on , entitled “Report on Facility Upgrades”.June 9, 2016

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: C Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the second target station under this paragraph occurs before , with the option for early operation in 2029.December 31, 2033

children.label: i
children.text: $127,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: ii

children.heading: Flux
children.label: i Flux
children.text: The term “flux” means the rate of flow of photons.

children.heading: Soft x-ray
children.label: ii Soft x-ray
children.text: The term “soft x-ray” means a photon with energy in the range from 50 to 2,000 electron volts.

children.heading: Upgrade
children.label: B Upgrade
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the upgrade to the Advanced Light Source described in the publication approved by the Basic Energy Sciences Advisory Committee on , entitled “Report on Facility Upgrades”, including the development of a multibend achromat lattice to produce a high flux of coherent x-rays within the soft x-ray energy region.June 9, 2016

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: C Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the upgrade under this paragraph occurs before .September 30, 2029

children.label: i
children.text: $135,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: ii
children.text: $102,500,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: iii
children.text: $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2025; and

children.label: iv
children.text: $1,400,000 for fiscal year 2026.

children.heading: Linac Coherent Light Source II high energy upgrade
children.label: 8 Linac Coherent Light Source II high energy upgrade

children.heading: High energy
children.label: i High energy
children.text: The term “high energy”, with respect to an x-ray, means a photon with an energy in the 5 to 13 kiloelectron volt range.

children.heading: High repetition rate
children.label: ii High repetition rate
children.text: The term “high repetition rate” means the delivery of x-ray pulses up to 1,000,000 pulses per second.

children.heading: Ultra-short pulse
children.label: iii Ultra-short pulse
children.text: The term “ultra-short pulse”, with respect to an x-ray, means that the x-ray has bursts capable of durations of less than 100 femtoseconds.

children.label: i
children.text: provide for the upgrade to the Linac Coherent Light Source II facility described in the publication approved by the Basic Energy Sciences Advisory Committee on , entitled “Report on Facility Upgrades”, including the development of experimental capabilities for high energy x-rays to reveal fundamental scientific discoveries; andJune 9, 2016

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure such upgrade enables the production and use of high energy, ultra-short pulse x-rays delivered at a high repetition rate.

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: C Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the upgrade under this paragraph occurs before .December 31, 2026

children.label: i
children.text: $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: ii
children.text: $130,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: iii
children.text: $135,000,000 for fiscal year 2025; and

children.label: iv
children.text: $99,343,000 for fiscal year 2026.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the construction of a cryomodule repair and maintenance facility to service the Linac Coherent Light Source II and subsequent upgrades.

children.heading: Consultation required
children.label: B Consultation required
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the private sector, institutions of higher education, National Laboratories, and relevant Federal agencies to ensure that the facility described in subparagraph (A) has the capability to maintain, repair, and test superconducting radio frequency accelerator components.

children.label: i
children.text: $29,300,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: iii

children.label: iv

children.heading: Nanoscale Science Research Center recapitalization project
children.label: 10 Nanoscale Science Research Center recapitalization project

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the recapitalization of the Nanoscale Science Research Centers, to include the upgrade of equipment at each Center supported by the Office of Science on , to accelerate advances in the various fields of science including nanoscience, materials, chemistry, biology, and quantum information science.August 9, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: $25,000,000 for fiscal year 2023; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $25,000,000 for fiscal year 2024.

children.heading: National Synchrotron Light Source II beamline buildout
children.label: 11 National Synchrotron Light Source II beamline buildout

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the development and construction of experimental stations to provide significant additional beamline and instrument capacity, complement the existing portfolio of beamlines, and complete the buildout of the National Synchrotron Light Source II.

children.label: i
children.text: shall begin carrying out subparagraph (A) not later than ; andSeptember 30, 2036

children.label: I
children.text: in calendar year 2033; or

children.label: II
children.text: after the construction of individual beamlines is complete.

children.heading: Accelerator research and development
children.label: e Accelerator research and development
children.text: The Director shall carry out research and development on advanced accelerator and storage ring technologies relevant to the development of basic energy sciences user facilities, in consultation with the High Energy Physics and Nuclear Physics programs of the Office of Science.

children.heading: Computational materials and chemical sciences
children.label: h Computational materials and chemical sciences
A
children.text: chemical catalysis research and development;

children.label: B
children.text: the use of large data sets to model materials phenomena, including through advanced characterization of materials, materials synthesis, processing, and innovative use of experimental and theoretical data;

children.label: C
children.text: codesign of chemical system and chemistry modeling software with advanced computing systems and hardware technologies; and

children.label: D
children.text: modeling of chemical processes, assemblies, and reactions such as molecular dynamics and quantum chemistry, including through novel computing methods.

children.heading: Computational materials and chemical sciences centers
children.label: 2 Computational materials and chemical sciences centers
i
children.text: develop open-source, robust, and validated computational codes and user-friendly software, coupled with innovative use of experimental and theoretical data, to enable the design, discovery, and development of new materials and chemical systems; and

children.label: ii
children.text: focus on overcoming challenges and maximizing the benefits of exascale and other high performance computing underpinned by accelerated node technologies.

children.heading: Selection
children.label: B Selection
children.text: The Director shall select centers under subparagraph (A) on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis. The Director shall consider applications from the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, multi-institutional collaborations, and other appropriate entities.

children.heading: New centers
children.label: i New centers
children.text: A center selected under subparagraph (A) shall receive support for a period of not more than 5 years beginning on the date of establishment of that center, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Existing centers
children.label: ii Existing centers
children.text: A center already in existence on , may continue to receive support for a period of not more than 5 years beginning on the date of establishment of that center.August 9, 2022

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: D Renewal
children.text: Upon the expiration of any period of support of a center under this subsection, the Director may renew support for the center, on a merit-reviewed basis, for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall support the development of a web-based platform to develop and provide access to a database of computed information on known and predicted materials properties and computational tools to accelerate breakthroughs in materials discovery and design.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct cooperative research among National Laboratories, industry, academia, and other research institutions to advance understanding, prediction, and manipulation of materials and facilitate the design of novel materials;

children.label: B
children.text: develop and maintain data infrastructure at user facilities that generate data to collect, analyze, label, and otherwise prepare the data for inclusion in the database;

children.label: C
children.text: leverage existing high performance computing systems to conduct high throughput calculations, and develop computational and data mining algorithms for the prediction of material properties;

children.label: D
children.text: strengthen the foundation for new technologies and advanced manufacturing; and

children.label: E
children.text: drive the development of advanced materials for applications that span the Department’s missions in energy, environment, and national security.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 3 Coordination
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Director shall leverage programs and activities across the Department, including computational materials and chemical sciences centers established under subsection (h).

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 4 Funding
children.text: Out of funds authorized to be appropriated under subsection (j), there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out activities under this subsection $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
$2,685,414,000 for fiscal year 2023;
$2,866,890,840 for fiscal year 2024;
$2,987,727,170 for fiscal year 2025;
$3,062,732,781 for fiscal year 2026; and
$3,080,067,167 for fiscal year 2027.
